text: Laparoscopic spleen preserving distal pancreatectomy. A thirty-six-year-old woman, presenting with epigastric fullness, abdominal CT revealed a multiloculated cyst at the tail of the pancreas.

The surgeon utilizes five laparoscopic ports, including two 12mm and three 5mm ports, to access the surgical field. The gastrocolic ligament is incised using a harmonic scalpel, revealing the underlying structures.
The surgeon makes an incision along the lower border of the pancreas to access the tumor situated at the tail of the pancreas.

The surgeon reflects the pancreas upward to expose the splenic vein at its upper border. Using a harmonic scalpel, the splenic vein is carefully dissected, maintaining a line of dissection a few millimeters away from the pancreatic vein within the pancreatic parenchyma to ensure hemostasis and prevent thermal injury.
The surgeon controls the splenic vein and artery with vessel loops, then divides the pancreas proximal to the tumor using a linear stapler with a triple staggered three-row configuration.
The surgeon completes the dissection of the remaining pancreas from the splenic vessels, marking the conclusion of the pancreatectomy procedure.
The histologic examination of the specimen revealed a serous cystadenoma of the pancreas. Thank you very much for your kind attention. Any questions will be more than welcome.
